steps.title: Read the shifted line
steps.content:
The graph shows $y=f(x)+11$. From the graph, two clear points on that line are $(-4,9)$ and $(0,1)$. The slope is
$$\frac{1-9}{0-(-4)}=-2$$
so the graphed line is
$$y=-2x+1$$

steps.title: Isolate the function
steps.content: Since $y=f(x)+11$, $$f(x)+11=-2x+1$$ $$f(x)=-2x-10$$ This has the form $-d-cx$ with positive constants $d=10$ and $c=2$. The answer is A.
